task_completion.description: The agent successfully navigates to the nodule location in 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible. The Red (axial) slice view shows a cross-section of the lung with a visible nodule (a distinct white opacity within the darker lung field).

task_completion.description: The agent activates the Markups tool to prepare for measurement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Markups' module is open in the side panel, or the 'Create LineMarkup' icon is active in the toolbar. A new markup list (e.g., 'Line-1') may be visible.

task_completion.description: The agent places a measurement line across the nodule's longest axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line markup is drawn across the nodule in the slice view. The endpoints of the line are positioned on the opposing edges of the nodule.

task_completion.description: The measurement value (in mm) is generated and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The length of the line is visible, either in the Markups module table (under the 'Length' column) or displayed as a text overlay next to the line in the viewer.

task_completion.description: The agent creates a JSON report with the measurement and correct Lung-RADS classification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displays the content of 'lungrads_report.json'. The 'measured_diameter_mm' matches the Slicer measurement, and 'lungrads_category' is correct based on the diameter (see privileged info).

task_completion.description: The agent saves the measurement markup and the report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog in Slicer is visible showing 'nodule_measurement.mrk.json' selected for saving, or a file explorer window shows both the measurement and report files in the correct directory.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI for navigation and measurement rather than scripting.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Slicer application window, buttons, and viewers, rather than exclusively typing commands into the Python Interactor or a terminal.

integrity.description: The reported measurement matches the visual measurement in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value written in 'lungrads_report.json' corresponds to the length shown in the Slicer Markups panel (allowing for minor rounding differences).

integrity.description: The agent does not attempt to look up ground truth data.
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browser is opened to search for LIDC nodule sizes or solution keys.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Lung-RADS Criteria for solid nodules: Category 2 (<6mm), Category 3 (6 to <8mm), Category 4A (8 to <15mm), Category 4B (>=15mm). Use these thresholds to verify if the 'lungrads_category' in the report matches the 'measured_diameter_mm'.
